---
title: "Maryam Mozafari: Transitioning from the oil & gas industry to renewable energy"
description: "Maryam will talk about her own experience transitioning from the oil & gas industry to renewable energy and then choosing climate change policy as a career."
image: /files/headshots/maryam-mozafari.webp
author: "Climate Guest Speakers"
date: "2024-10-15"
categories: [Climate and Career]
#page-layout: full
#bibliography: references.bib
#csl: chicago-syllabus.csl
#suppress-bibliography: true
#link-citations: false
#citations-hover: false
listing:  #an example of listing external links
#  template: item-default-external.ejs
- id: speaker
  sort: "date asc"
  type: grid
  fields: [image, title, subtitle]
  grid-item-align: center
  grid-columns: 2
  image-height: 300px
#  categories: true
#  sort-ui: true
#  filter-ui: true
  contents:
    - path: https://www.linkedin.com/in/maryam-mozafari #linkedin profile or personal website
      image: /files/headshots/maryam-mozafari.webp
      title: "Maryam Mozafari"
#      author: 
      subtitle: "Program Supervisor, California Public Utilities Commission"
#      date: "2024-03-12"
#      categories: [] 
page-navigation: false
title-block-banner: false
#toc: false
---

## Title


### Time

Tuesday, October 15, 2024, 6:00PM - 7:00PM

### Venue 
Online via zoom. Please [register](https://forms.gle/65hnhJPmnYaz5utG9) to participate.

## About

Maryam will share her work at CPUC and talk about her own experience transitioning from the oil & gas industry to renewable energy and then choosing climate change policy as a career. 

## Speaker

:::{#speaker}
:::


### Bio

Maryam leads the Demand Response/Demand Flexibility section at the California Public Utilities Commission. Her team develops policies aimed at unlocking the potential of demand flexibility (from distribution level DR to CAISO wholesale integration to CalFUSE) and maximizing the utilization of Behind-the-Meter and Distributed Energy Resources. Maryam previously served as Senior Energy Advisor to Commissioner Darcie Houck where she worked on innovative strategies for the planning and operation of a high DER distribution grid.  Whether a manager, an advisor, or an engineer on offshore oil platforms, Maryam aspires to make the world a better place, one step at a time. She holds a BSc in Mechanical Engineering and a MSc in Energy and Resources from UC Berkeley.



<!--Include social share buttons-->

{{< include /files/includes/_socialshare.qmd >}}
